task_completion.description: Navigate to the space properties for the Top Floor spaces in eQUEST.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST interface shows the building tree or spreadsheet view with spaces starting with 'T.' (e.g., T. South, T. East, T. North, T. West, T. Core) selected or visible.

task_completion.description: Locate and edit the 'FLOOR-WEIGHT' parameter for a Top Floor space.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'FLOOR-WEIGHT' parameter field or spreadsheet cell is visible in the eQUEST interface and is being changed to 120.

task_completion.description: Ensure the 'FLOOR-WEIGHT' parameter is updated to 120 for all 5 Top Floor spaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet view, component tree, or multiple screenshots showing the 'FLOOR-WEIGHT' value set to 120 (or within 115-125) for all 5 spaces starting with 'T.'.

task_completion.description: Run the full annual DOE-2.2 simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The eQUEST simulation dialog box is open, showing a running progress bar or a 'Simulation Complete' message indicating the DOE-2.2 simulation was executed.
The agent clicks the 'Save' icon (floppy disk) in the toolbar, uses File > Save in the menu, or a 'Project Saved' confirmation is visible.

integrity.description: The agent used the eQUEST GUI to make changes instead of editing text files.
integrity.visual_evidence: All parameter modifications occur within eQUEST's dialog boxes or spreadsheet views, not in Notepad, VSCode, or another text editor modifying the .inp file directly.

integrity.description: The simulation was actually run through the eQUEST interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The simulation progress or completion dialog is the native eQUEST window, not a terminal script or mocked output.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open where the agent is using commands like 'sed' or Python scripts to mass-replace 'FLOOR-WEIGHT' values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target building model is '4StoreyBuilding'. The target spaces to update start with the prefix 'T.' (exactly 5 spaces: South, East, North, West, Core). The target parameter to update is 'FLOOR-WEIGHT'. The target value is 120 (acceptable tolerance is +/- 5, i.e., 115-125).
